name: vscode-tapsense
description: Detects single, double, and triple tap gestures on your MacBook via accelerometer sidecar

tapsense.sidecarPath.description: Path to the tapsense-sidecar binary. If empty, uses the built binary in the repo.

tapsense.simulateMode.description: Run sidecar in --simulate mode (no real accelerometer needed).

tapsense.mode.description: Detection profile to pass to the sidecar.

tapsense.sensitivity.description: Detection sensitivity to pass to the sidecar.

tapsense.keySuppressionMs.description: Skip samples within this many milliseconds AFTER a key or mouse event.

tapsense.keyLookaheadMs.description: Delay emit this many milliseconds — cancel if any key/mouse event fires during window.

tapsense.autoStart.description: Automatically start listening on extension activation.

tapsense.singleTap.command.description: VS Code command id to run on a single tap. Can target another extension's command.

tapsense.singleTap.args.description: Arguments passed to the single-tap command.

tapsense.singleTap.showNotification.description: Show a VS Code notification when a single tap is detected.

tapsense.doubleTap.command.description: VS Code command id to run on a double tap. Can target another extension's command.

tapsense.doubleTap.args.description: Arguments passed to the double-tap command.

tapsense.doubleTap.showNotification.description: Show a VS Code notification when a double tap is detected.

tapsense.tripleTap.command.description: VS Code command id to run on a triple tap. Can target another extension's command.

tapsense.tripleTap.args.description: Arguments passed to the triple-tap command.

tapsense.tripleTap.showNotification.description: Show a VS Code notification when a triple tap is detected.
